score_injection_action: Rank=Disgraced/<0 with close_fail_qa_pass(-12) in recent events signals pattern of over-permissive passing; this run zeroes in on whether the two specific pass-2 blocking locations (ticket line 53, line 114) are truly fixed and also independently scans the whole ticket for any new residual or introduced drift — not just accepting BA's self-reported fix scope

qa.overall_assessment: OBJ-3 fully resolved: both blocking locations have been corrected. Line 53 (Dev Constraints arch-8) now explicitly says 'NOT blast-radius-tool.py:35-38, which contains only filesystem-noise patterns'. Line 114 (R10 Requirements Decomposition table) now correctly distinguishes sensitive-data patterns (cited to spec §5 Risk-7 + arch-8) from filesystem-noise patterns (cited to blast-radius-tool.py:35-38). The phrase 'mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35' appears ONLY in the HTML comment on line 202 (the OBJ-3 fix annotation, which the task brief explicitly permits). All 5 pass-1/pass-2 objections are now resolved. One minor scope gap is noted as an out-of-scope observation: the Requirements Decomposition table stops at R11 but context JSON has R12 — however, AC12 exists in the Acceptance Criteria section covering the same content, so no AC coverage is lost.

objections.title: AC2 fail gates removed (RESOLVED — confirmed pass3)
objections.description: AC2 THEN clause correctly reads: agents/ba.md contains the 6-step Reference Resolution procedure AND the list of implicit reference trigger words. NOTE clause explicitly directs fail gates to AC3 only. No regression.
objections.evidence: ticket lines 141-148: THEN clause without fail gates; NOTE clause explicit about AC3-only assignment.

objections.title: AC5 third registration site added (RESOLVED — confirmed pass3)
objections.description: AC5 requires 'graphify' in CP_AGENTS (pretool-cp-checkin.py:~72), ALLOWED_AGENTS (spec-check.py:~59), and agent_types list (hooks/prompt-workflow.py:598-603). Sentinel creation consequence documented. No regression.
objections.evidence: ticket lines 162-169 confirmed; all three registration sites explicitly required.

objections.title: False 'mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35' attribution FULLY REMOVED from all live spec text (RESOLVED)
objections.description: Pass 2 identified two remaining live locations: (1) line 53 Dev Constraints and (2) line 114 R10 table. Both have now been corrected. Line 53 now reads: 'Arch-8 MANDATES sensitive data exclusion enforced in graphify_lib.py using EXCLUDE_FRAGMENTS tuple; the authority for sensitive-data patterns (.env, credentials, keys, logs) is spec Section 5 Risk item 7 and arch-8 — NOT blast-radius-tool.py:35-38, which contains only filesystem-noise patterns (/venv/, /worktrees/, /.git/, /__pycache__/, /node_modules/).' Line 114 now reads: 'graphify_lib.py defines EXCLUDE_FRAGMENTS tuple with sensitive-data patterns (.env, credentials, keys, logs) as required by spec Section 5 Risk item 7 and arch-8; filesystem-noise patterns (/venv/, /.git/, etc.) may be drawn from blast-radius-tool.py:35-38'. Both locations correctly distinguish the two authorities. The phrase 'mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35' appears only in the HTML comment on line 202 (OBJ-3 fix annotation), which the task brief explicitly marks as acceptable. grep for 'mirroring blast-radius-tool' in ticket returns only line 202 (comment).
objections.evidence: grep -n 'mirroring blast-radius-tool' ticket: only line 202 (HTML comment). sed -n '53p': NOT blast-radius-tool.py:35-38 phrase confirmed. sed -n '114p': sensitive-data cited to spec §5 Risk-7 + arch-8; noise patterns attributed to blast-radius-tool.py:35-38 (correct constrained attribution). No live spec text carries the false attribution.

objections.title: commands/dev-overnight.md added to PR2 affected files (RESOLVED — confirmed pass3)
objections.description: commands/dev-overnight.md appears in PR2 affected-files list in both ticket Technical Hints (line 251) and context JSON files_to_modify. The minor inaccuracy in OBJ-4 investigation (characterizing lines 248-263 as 'literal enumeration' rather than 'example template blocks') is noted but does not create a missing requirement. The OBJ-4 investigation conclusion correctly identifies the required modification: add 'graphify' examples to the Form A/Form B template code blocks in dev-overnight.md. objections.evidence: ticket line 251 confirmed: 'commands/dev-overnight.md (modify — add graphify to Step 1 sentinel fanout list...)'. context JSON files_to_modify includes 'commands/dev-overnight.md'.

evidence_quality.notes: All OBJ fix claims in BA's pass-3 fix are evidence-backed. BA cited specific file/line evidence for both OBJ-3 locations. The OBJ-3 fix comment (line 202) documents the Read of blast-radius-tool.py:35-38 confirming absence of .env/credentials/keys/logs patterns. Both corrections are substantiated.

scope_alignment.notes: All 12 ACs (AC1-AC12) cover the spec Section 5 requirements: dual-touchpoint architecture, Reference Resolution, BA-QA fail gates, advisory distinction, agent registration, integer step numbering, file locking, nil-map fallback, state machine completeness, sensitive data exclusion, no-op guard, Command Coverage table. Since AC12 exists in the Acceptance Criteria section with full spec-coverage, this is a table-completeness minor gap, not a missing AC. Routed to out_of_scope_observations.
